golden_categories.name: Standard FQDN Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates fully qualified domain names (FQDN) with default options.

golden_categories.name: Trailing Dot Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow a trailing dot.

golden_categories.name: No TLD Requirement
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs without requiring a top-level domain (TLD).

golden_categories.name: Numeric TLDs Without Requirement
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with numeric TLDs when TLD requirement is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Wildcard Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow wildcard domains.

golden_categories.name: Combined Options
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with multiple options enabled: trailing dot, underscores, and numeric TLDs.

generated_categories.name: Default Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests the default behavior of isFQDN with common valid and invalid cases.

generated_categories.name: TLD Requirement Options
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior when the requirement for a top-level domain (TLD) is modified.

generated_categories.name: Underscore Allowance
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior when underscores are allowed in domain parts.

generated_categories.name: Trailing Dot Allowance
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior when trailing dots are allowed in domain names.

generated_categories.name: Numeric TLD Allowance
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior when numeric top-level domains (TLDs) are allowed.

generated_categories.name: Wildcard Domain Allowance
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior when wildcard domains are allowed.

golden_to_generated.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 as both focus on standard FQDN validation with default options.
GENC-4 aligns with GC-2 as both test the allowance of trailing dots in FQDNs.
GENC-2 partially aligns with GC-3 as it tests the modification of TLD requirements, but it does not explicitly cover numeric TLDs.
GENC-5 aligns with GC-4 as both test the allowance of numeric TLDs when TLD requirements are disabled.
GENC-6 aligns with GC-5 as both test the allowance of wildcard domains in FQDNs.
No generated category combines multiple options (trailing dot, underscores, numeric TLDs) as described in GC-6.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 as both focus on standard FQDN validation with default options.
GENC-2 partially aligns with GC-3 as it tests the modification of TLD requirements, but it does not explicitly cover numeric TLDs.
GENC-3 focuses on underscore allowance, which is not covered by any golden category.
GENC-4 aligns with GC-2 as both test the allowance of trailing dots in FQDNs.
GENC-5 aligns with GC-4 as both test the allowance of numeric TLDs when TLD requirements are disabled.
GENC-6 aligns with GC-5 as both test the allowance of wildcard domains in FQDNs.

novel_generated_categories.reason: GENC-3 introduces underscore allowance, which is not addressed by any golden category.

missing_golden_categories.reason: No generated categories achieved strong or partial semantic overlap with this fixed golden category.
